mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously when rates are in the easing zone.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 2.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150,000 persons, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts below 1,200 thousand units signals weakening demand expectations and reduced future construction employment, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST < 1,200 thousand, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization. This mechanism predicts negative 5-day returns as investors reprrice near-term earnings expectations downward in response to manufacturing slowdown.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where INDPRO growth < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales and real consumer spending (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling a demand shock and labor market deterioration. This joint deterioration triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprrice recession risk.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline > 1% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where both RSAFS declines > 1% and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) continues to accelerate month-over-month above 0.4%, the Fed is in restrictive tightening mode. This joint condition signals sustained real rate pressure that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where both FEDFUNDS > 3.5% and CPIAUCSL growth > 0.4%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 3.5% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) continues to grow, signaling a tight labor market with wage pressure and potential inflation acceleration. In this regime, equity investors demand higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage inflation risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE < 3.5% and PAYEMS growth > 100,000, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,300 thousand units, signaling synchronized demand weakness across consumption and investment. This joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprrice long-term growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 1.5% AND HOUST < 1,300 thousand units
regime_hint: demand contraction or late-cycle slowdown with synchronized weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where GDPC1 growth < 1.5% and HOUST < 1,300 thousand, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
